exercises.name: Dumbbell Alternating Reverse Lunge with Bicep Curl
exercises.description: Step back into a reverse lunge with one leg while curling the dumbbells to your shoulders at the same time. Stand up and repeat on the other leg, alternating sides.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Alternating Shoulder Press
exercises.description:
1) When performing the shoulder press, as you extend each arm up, ensure that your arm is in line with your ear. 

2) Hold a dumbbell in each hand. 

3) Raise the dumbbells to your shoulders with your palms facing forward and your elbows pointed out. This will be your starting position. 

4) Extend one arm to press the dumbbell straight up, keeping your off hand in place. 

5) After a brief pause, return the weight to the starting position. 

6) Repeat for the opposite side, continuing to alternate between arms.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Alternating Tricep Press
exercises.alternateName: Flat Bench Narrow Alternating Chest Press
exercises.description:
Perform a narrow chest press on a flat bench, but alternate arms with each rep. 

This increases the challenge on your core while focusing on the triceps and inner chest.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Atlas Press
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright holding one dumbbell in both hands at one shoulder with your elbows bent.

2.Press the dumbbell overhead, extending your arms fully

3.Lower the dumbbell down to the other side to shoulder height.

4.Repeat pressing the dumbbell up and over back to the start position. Alternate sides with each rep.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Deadbug Pullover
exercises.description:
Lie on a bench holding a single dumbbell with both hands, arms fully extended above your chest, palms facing each other. Lift your legs so your knees are bent at 90 degrees and your shins are parallel to the floor (deadbug position). 

Slowly lower one leg toward the floor while simultaneously lowering the dumbbell back over your head toward the bench. Bring the leg and dumbbell back to the starting position, then repeat with the opposite leg. 

Continue alternating legs for the desired number of reps while keeping your core engaged and hips stable.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.description:
1) Lay on a flat bench with dumbbells in each hand. 

2) Bend your elbows at a 90-degree angle so that your forearms are perpendicular to the ground.

3) Press the weights upward until the two weights meet at the top. 

4) Pause and lower back to the start position.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bent over Low Row to High Row
exercises.description:
Stand with your feet hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand. Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is almost parallel to the floor, keeping a slight bend in your knees and your back straight. 

Perform a low row by pulling the dumbbells toward your lower ribs, squeezing your shoulder blades together. Then transition into a high row by pulling the dumbbells toward your upper chest/shoulders, driving your elbows upward and outward.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bent Over Row
exercises.description:
1)  When performing this exercise, maintain a safe back by arching or keeping a neutral spine throughout the exercise. 

2) The position will similar to that of the deadlift. 

3) Grab the dumbbell and pull it to your stomach, retracting your shoulder blade and flexing the elbow. 

4) Lower and Repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bent over row to Rear Fly
exercises.description:
Stand with your feet hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand. 

Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is almost parallel to the floor, keeping a slight bend in your knees and your back straight. 

Row the dumbbells toward your ribcage, squeezing your shoulder blades together, then lower the dumbbells and immediately perform a rear lateral raise (rear fly) by lifting your arms out to the sides until they reach shoulder height.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bent Over Row to Tricep Kickback
exercises.description: Using dumbbells, from a hinge, row the weights toward your ribs, then extend your elbows back in a tricep kickback.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bicep Curl
exercises.description:
1) Stand up straight with arms hanging and dumbbells in both hands, palms facing in. 

2) Curl dumbbells for both arms at once, twisting your wrists on the way up and keeping your elbows still. 

3) Slowly lower repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell B‑Stance Floor Glute Bridge
exercises.description:
Lie on your back with one foot flat on the floor and the other foot slightly back (B‑stance). 

Hold a dumbbell on your hips with both hands for added resistance. Press through the heel of the front foot to lift your hips off the floor, squeezing your glutes at the top while keeping your back straight. 

Slowly lower your hips back down to the floor and repeat for reps.

exercises.name: Dumbbell B‑Stance Front Squat (Double Rack)
exercises.description:
Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand in a front rack position at your shoulders, palms facing inward. Position one foot slightly forward and the other slightly back (B‑stance), keeping most of your weight on the front leg. 

Lower your body by bending your knees and pushing your hips back, keeping your chest upright and elbows high. Push through your front heel to return to standing.

exercises.name: Dumbbell B‑Stance Goblet Squat
exercises.description:
Stand tall holding a single dumbbell close to your chest with both hands (goblet position). 

Position one foot slightly forward and the other slightly back (B‑stance), keeping most of your weight on the front leg. 

Lower your body by bending your knees and pushing your hips back, keeping your chest upright and dumbbell close to your chest. 

Push through your front heel to return to standing.

exercises.name: Dumbbell B‑Stance RDL to Row
exercises.description:
Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand in front of your thighs. Position one foot slightly forward and the other slightly back (B‑stance), keeping most of your weight on the front leg. 

Hinge at the hips and lower the dumbbells toward the floor, maintaining a slight bend in your knees and a straight back (RDL). From the bottom of the hinge, pull the dumbbells toward your torso by bending your elbows and squeezing your shoulder blades together (row). 

Lower the dumbbells back down while maintaining the hip hinge, then return to standing.

exercises.name: Dumbbell B-Stance RDL to Single Arm Row
exercises.alternateName: kick stand RDL to single arm row
exercises.description:
Stand tall holding a dumbbell in one hand in front of your thighs. Position the foot on the same side as the dumbbell slightly back and the opposite foot slightly forward (B‑stance), keeping most of your weight on the front leg. 

Hinge at the hips and lower the dumbbell toward the floor, maintaining a slight bend in your knees and a straight back. From the bottom of the hinge, row the dumbbell toward your torso by bending your elbow and squeezing your shoulder blade. 

Lower the dumbbell back down while maintaining the hinge, then return to standing.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bulgarian Split Squat
exercises.alternateName: Bulgarian rear foot elevated single leg
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, ensure that your knee, feet and hip are in line. 

2) With your feet hip-width apart, place the instep of your rear foot on a bench. Your feet should be approximately three feet apart. 

3) Lower your hips toward the floor so that your rear knee comes close to the floor. 

4) Pause and drive through your front heel to return to the starting position. 

5) Repeat

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bulgarian Split Squat to Curl
exercises.description:
Stand in a split stance with your rear foot elevated on a bench or platform and your front foot on the floor. 

Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides. Lower your body into a Bulgarian split squat by bending your front knee and keeping your torso upright. 

As you push through the front heel to return to standing, perform a bicep curl with the dumbbells.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bulgarian Split Squat to Single Arm Curl
exercises.description:
Stand in a split stance with your rear foot elevated on a bench or platform and your front foot on the floor. 

Hold a dumbbell in the hand opposite to the front leg at your side. Lower your body into a Bulgarian split squat by bending your front knee and keeping your torso upright. 

As you push through the front heel to return to standing, perform a bicep curl with the dumbbell.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Burpee Clean to Press
exercises.description: Place the dumbbells on the floor, jump your feet back into a plank, then jump forward to stand. Clean the dumbbells to your shoulders, then press them overhead.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Burpee to Overhead Swing
exercises.description: Place the dumbbells on the floor, jump your feet back into a plank, then jump forward to stand. Swing the weight to shoulder height with a hip hinge.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Burpee with Curl to Press
exercises.description: Place the dumbbells on the floor, jump your feet back into a plank, then jump forward to stand. Curl the dumbbells to your shoulders, then press them overhead.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Calf Raise
exercises.description:
1) When performing a calf raise, ensure that your knees are straight and try to get the full range of motion through your ankle. 

2) Start by grasping a dumbbell in your left hand and standing on the edge of an elevated platform. Let both your heel drop as far as possible. 

3) Keeping your body straight, raise both heels up as far as possible. 

4) Pause and squeeze the calf muscle, and then slowly lower your heel back down as far as possible. 

5) Repeat for desired reps.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Clean and Jerk
exercises.description:
1.Squat holding a dumbbell in each hand between your feet with your arms straight.

2.Thrust your hips forward pulling the dumbbells straight up, catching them at shoulder height, dipping at the hips and knees slightly.

3.Quickly press the dumbbells overhead splitting your feet to absorb them at the top.

4.Return to starting position and repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Clean and Press
exercises.alternateName: DB high pull to shoulder press
exercises.description:
1.Squat holding a dumbbell in each hand at your shins with your arms straight.

2.Thrust your hips forward and pull the dumbbells straight up, catch them at shoulder height, dipping at the hips and knees slightly.

3.Quickly push the dumbbells overhead, extending your arms and legs fully.

4.Return to starting position and repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Clean to Press
exercises.description:
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides. 

Hinge slightly at the hips and bend your knees, then perform a clean by explosively pulling the dumbbells from your sides up to shoulder height. 

Immediately press the dumbbells overhead, extending your arms fully. Lower the dumbbells back to the starting position and repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Concentration Curl
exercises.description:
1) Sit on the end of a flat bench with your legs spread apart in a V-shape. 

2) Rest your elbow on the inside of your thigh letting the dumbbell hang, while your other hand rests on the upper thigh of the other leg. 

3) Slowly curl the weight up towards your shoulder using just your bicep. 

4) Pause at the top and slowly lower to start position.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Curl to Shoulder Press
exercises.description: Using dumbbells, curl the weights to your shoulders, then press the weights overhead.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Curtsy Lunge to Lateral Raise
exercises.alternateName: Dumbbell Curtsy Lunge to side Raise
exercises.description:
Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand by your sides. Step one leg diagonally behind the other into a curtsy lunge, lowering your hips until your front thigh is nearly parallel to the floor. 

Push through your front heel to return to standing. As you rise, lift both arms out to your sides until they reach shoulder height for a lateral raise. 

Lower the dumbbells back down to your sides and repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Deadlift
exercises.description:
1) Stand and hold two dumbbells with palms facing in.

2) With your back straight and knees locked, bend your body at your waist and lower down to the floor. 

3) Then straighten up while still holding the dumbbells at arms length. This is a very difficult exercise and is not meant for beginners.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Deadlift to Alternating Reverse Lunge
exercises.description: Using dumbbells, hinge to lower the weights and stand tall, then alternate stepping back into reverse lunges.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Deadlift to Bent-Over Row
exercises.description: Using dumbbells, hinge to lower the weights and stand tall, then from a hinge, row the weights toward your ribs.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Deadlift to Curl
exercises.description: Using dumbbells, hinge to lower the weights and stand tall, then curl the weights to your shoulders.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Deadlift to Curl to Press
exercises.description: Using dumbbells, hinge to lower the weights and stand tall, then curl the weights to your shoulders, then press the weights overhead.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Deadlift to Row
exercises.description:
Stand with feet hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand in front of your thighs. Hinge at the hips to lower the dumbbells toward the floor, keeping your back flat and chest up. 

From the lowered position, perform a row by pulling the dumbbells toward your torso, elbows close to your body. 

Lower the dumbbells back toward the floor while maintaining a neutral spine, then return to standing to repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Deadlift to Upright Row
exercises.description: Using dumbbells, hinge to lower the weights and stand tall, then pull the weights straight up to chest height (upright row).

exercises.name: Dumbbell Decline Bench Chest Fly
exercises.description:
1) Lie on a decline bench holding the dumbbells up over your chest with your arms straight.

2) Lower dumbbells outwards in a semicircular motion and down to shoulder level.

3) Raise the dumbbells back up over your chest, returning to starting position, keeping your arms straight.

4) Repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Decline Bench Press
exercises.description:
1) Lie on a decline bench with two dumbbells. 

2) Start with dumbbells positioned just below nipples.

3) Raise dumbbells all the way up, pause, and lower weight. Make sure to keep your back flat on the bench at all times.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Deficit Reverse Lunge
exercises.description: Dumbbell Deficit Reverse Lunge

exercises.name: Dumbbell Eccentric Step Up
exercises.description:
Stand facing a sturdy bench or step, holding a dumbbell in each hand by your sides. Step one foot onto the bench and slowly drive through your heel to lift your body upward, taking 3–5 seconds to rise. 

Once fully on the bench, slowly lower back down to the starting position over 3–5 seconds, maintaining control throughout. Keep your torso upright and core engaged.
